# Marker

Displays an inline status, system note, bordered row, or labeled separator in a conversation.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Composition](#composition)
- [Features](#features)
- [Examples](#examples)
- [Accessibility](#accessibility)
- [Content and state](#content-and-state)
- [When to use](#when-to-use)
- [API Reference](#api-reference)

## Installation

Add Marker from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/marker
```

## Usage

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex w-full max-w-sm flex-col gap-8 py-6">
    <Marker>
      <MarkerIcon>
        <GitBranchIcon />
      </MarkerIcon>
      <MarkerContent>Switched to a new branch</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>

    <Marker role="status">
      <MarkerIcon>
        <Spinner />
      </MarkerIcon>
      <MarkerContent>Thinking…</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>

    <Marker variant="separator">
      <MarkerContent>Conversation compacted</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>

    <Marker>
      <MarkerIcon>
        <SearchIcon />
      </MarkerIcon>
      <MarkerContent>Explored 4 files</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { GitBranchIcon, SearchIcon } from '@lucide/vue'
import { Marker, MarkerContent, MarkerIcon } from '@/Components/Marker'
import { Spinner } from '@/Components/Spinner'
</script>
```

## Composition

Compose the root with an optional decorative icon and a content slot. The root forwards primitive props, so it can render another element or adopt a child with `as-child`.

```text
Marker
├── MarkerIcon
└── MarkerContent
```

## Features

- Default, bordered, and labeled-separator variants.
- A decorative icon slot hidden from assistive technology.
- Polymorphic rendering through `as` and `as-child`.
- Semantic roles chosen by the consumer for each use case.

## Examples

### Variants

Use the default marker for notes, separator for labeled divisions, and border for row boundaries.

**Variants example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex w-full max-w-sm flex-col gap-8 py-6">
    <Marker>
      <MarkerContent>A default marker for inline notes.</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
    <Marker variant="separator">
      <MarkerContent>A separator marker</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
    <Marker variant="border">
      <MarkerContent>A bordered marker for row boundaries.</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Marker, MarkerContent } from '@/Components/Marker'
</script>
```

### Status

Add `role="status"` when the marker reports a live, non-urgent update.

**Status example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex w-full max-w-sm flex-col gap-8 py-6">
    <Marker role="status">
      <MarkerIcon>
        <Spinner />
      </MarkerIcon>
      <MarkerContent>Compacting conversation</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
    <Marker variant="separator" role="status">
      <MarkerIcon>
        <Spinner />
      </MarkerIcon>
      <MarkerContent>Running tests</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Marker, MarkerContent, MarkerIcon } from '@/Components/Marker'
import { Spinner } from '@/Components/Spinner'
</script>
```

### Shimmer

Marker content can carry an optional text animation for ongoing work. Keep a reduced-motion fallback when adding this effect.

**Shimmer example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex w-full max-w-sm flex-col gap-8 py-6">
    <Marker role="status">
      <MarkerContent class="marker-shimmer">
        Thinking…
      </MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
    <Marker variant="separator" role="status">
      <MarkerContent class="marker-shimmer">
        Reading 4 files
      </MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Marker, MarkerContent } from '@/Components/Marker'
</script>

<style scoped>
.marker-shimmer {
  color: transparent;
  background-image: linear-gradient(
    90deg,
    var(--muted-foreground) 35%,
    var(--foreground) 50%,
    var(--muted-foreground) 65%
  );
  background-size: 200% 100%;
  background-clip: text;
  animation: marker-shimmer 2s linear infinite;
}

@keyframes marker-shimmer {
  to {
    background-position: -200% 0;
  }
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .marker-shimmer {
    animation: none;
  }
}
</style>
```

### Separator

Use a labeled separator to divide a timeline or conversation without adding a separator role.

**Separator example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex w-full max-w-sm flex-col gap-8 py-6">
    <Marker variant="separator">
      <MarkerContent>Today</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
    <Marker variant="separator">
      <MarkerContent>Worked for 42 seconds</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
    <Marker variant="separator">
      <MarkerContent>Conversation compacted</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Marker, MarkerContent } from '@/Components/Marker'
</script>
```

### Border

The border variant adds a decorative bottom rule while retaining the marker's chosen semantics.

**Border example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex w-full max-w-sm flex-col gap-3 py-6">
    <Marker variant="border">
      <MarkerIcon>
        <GitBranchIcon />
      </MarkerIcon>
      <MarkerContent>Switched to release-candidate</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
    <Marker variant="border">
      <MarkerIcon>
        <SearchIcon />
      </MarkerIcon>
      <MarkerContent>Reviewed 8 related files</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
    <Marker variant="border">
      <MarkerIcon>
        <FileTextIcon />
      </MarkerIcon>
      <MarkerContent>Opened implementation notes</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { FileTextIcon, GitBranchIcon, SearchIcon } from '@lucide/vue'
import { Marker, MarkerContent, MarkerIcon } from '@/Components/Marker'
</script>
```

### With icon

Place an icon inside `MarkerIcon`; adjacent text should communicate the same meaning.

**With icon example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex w-full max-w-sm flex-col gap-10 py-6">
    <Marker>
      <MarkerIcon>
        <GitBranchIcon />
      </MarkerIcon>
      <MarkerContent>Switched to a new branch</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
    <Marker variant="separator">
      <MarkerIcon>
        <SearchIcon />
      </MarkerIcon>
      <MarkerContent>Explored 4 files</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
    <Marker class="flex-col">
      <MarkerIcon>
        <BookOpenCheckIcon />
      </MarkerIcon>
      <MarkerContent>Sync completed</MarkerContent>
    </Marker>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { BookOpenCheckIcon, GitBranchIcon, SearchIcon } from '@lucide/vue'
import { Marker, MarkerContent, MarkerIcon } from '@/Components/Marker'
</script>
```

### Links and buttons

Use `as-child` when a marker is interactive so the rendered element remains a real link or button.

**Links and buttons example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="flex w-full max-w-sm flex-col gap-8 py-6">
    <Marker as-child>
      <a href="#links-and-buttons">
        <MarkerIcon>
          <GitBranchIcon />
        </MarkerIcon>
        <MarkerContent>View the pull request</MarkerContent>
      </a>
    </Marker>
    <Marker as-child>
      <button
        type="button"
        class="transition-colors hover:text-foreground"
        @click="reverted = true"
      >
        <MarkerIcon>
          <RotateCcwIcon />
        </MarkerIcon>
        <MarkerContent>{{ reverted ? 'Change reverted' : 'Revert this change' }}</MarkerContent>
      </button>
    </Marker>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { GitBranchIcon, RotateCcwIcon } from '@lucide/vue'
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { Marker, MarkerContent, MarkerIcon } from '@/Components/Marker'

const reverted = ref(false)
</script>
```

## Accessibility

Marker is presentational by default. Add `role="status"` for live progress, keep meaningful labels as ordinary content, and avoid `role="separator"` on separators that contain text.

`MarkerIcon` is always decorative. Provide visible `MarkerContent` or an accessible label when an icon is the only visible child.

## Content and state

Marker presents content supplied by its parent. Keep timestamps, progress text, status changes, and link destinations in the owning feature; choose `role="status"` only when a newly rendered non-urgent update should be announced. A shimmer is visual feedback, not a replacement for a concrete progress label.

## When to use

- Use Marker for short conversation notes, timeline boundaries, and inline system status.
- Use Separator for an unlabeled structural boundary outside a conversation or timeline.
- Use Alert when the message needs stronger visual priority or dedicated action content.
- Avoid Marker for persistent record metadata that belongs in Item, Panel, or Table.

## API Reference

### Marker

The root marker element. It also exports `markerVariants`.

- `variant`: `default`, `separator`, or `border`.
- `as`: the element or component to render; defaults to `div`.
- `as-child`: merges Marker behavior into its single child.
- `class`: additional classes merged with the selected variant.

### MarkerIcon

A decorative icon container with `aria-hidden="true"`.

### MarkerContent

The marker's text content, including link styling and separator alignment.

Hosted documentation: [Marker](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/marker)
